name: loopy
description: A small skill system for counterexample-driven improvement, code-structure recomposition, and evidence-based project theory.

interface.longDescription: Use Loopy to define done, find the strongest useful counterexample, make the smallest improvement, check the result, and keep looping until all important in-scope counterexamples are resolved, blocked, or out of scope. Use loopy-compose to apply the loop to code-structure recomposition. Use loopy-theory to infer an evidence-based ideal theory from a project's current traces and open improvement candidates.
defaultPrompt: Use loopy to improve this through counterexamples.
Use loopy to keep iterating until all important in-scope counterexamples are resolved, blocked, or out of scope.
Use loopy-compose to recompose code structure through the loopy improvement loop.
Use loopy-theory to infer the ideal theory suggested by this project's current evidence and identify next improvement candidates.
